name: Moreruela Monastery and Castrotorafe Castle Loop
name_original: Moreruela de Tábara desde Granja de Moreruela – circular por el Castillo Castrotorafe
description: Cycle a moderate 34.0-mile circular route from Granja de Moreruela, exploring historical ruins and rural landscapes in Zamora province.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Benavente Y Los Valles traverse a landscape characterized by fertile river valleys, including the Tera, Órbigo, Esla, Eria, and Cea rivers. The region features undulating hills and expansive open fields, particularly in the plains of Zamora. Cyclists can encounter natural reserve lagoons and oak forests, offering varied scenery. Best touring cycling routes around Benavente Y Los Valles The most popular touring cycling route is Landscaping from Benavente to Morales del Río — Benavente y los Valles, a 30.2 miles (48.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 22 minutes to complete. This route offers a challenging ride through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Vía de la Plata Greenway Loop, an easy 10.2 miles (16.4 km) path. This route follows a section of the historic Vía de la Plata, providing a gentle ride. Local touring cyclists also love the Rodeando el río Esla desde Benavente — Benavente, a 12.7 miles (20.5 km) trail leading through the scenic Río Esla valley, often completed in about 1 hour 24 minutes. Touring cycling around Benavente Y Los Valles is defined by river valleys, undulating hills, and open fields. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides on paved surfaces to more challenging paths. The routes in Benavente Y Los Valles are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 150 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Benavente Y Los Valles's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Benavente y Los Valles?
A: There are over 40 touring cycling routes recorded in Benavente y Los Valles, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored these routes over 150 times, providing valuable insights and ratings.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Benavente y Los Valles?
A: The region is characterized by fertile river valleys, including the Tera, Órbigo, Esla, Eria, and Cea rivers, which often feature lush scenery and waterside paths. You'll also encounter undulating hills and expansive open fields, particularly in the plains of Zamora. Routes can range from easy, mostly paved surfaces to more challenging paths that may include unpaved sections.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Benavente y Los Valles offers several easy routes. For a gentle ride along a historic path, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383208256/via-de-la-plata-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt;. Another accessible option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383246378/rodeando-el-rio-esla-desde-benavente-benavente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rodeando el río Esla desde Benavente — Benavente&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the scenic Río Esla valley.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383991697/landscaping-from-benavente-to-morales-del-rio-benavente-y-los-valles" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Landscaping from Benavente to Morales del Río — Benavente y los Valles&lt;/a&gt; offer varied terrain and significant elevation changes. Another demanding option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037591117/benavente-pobladura-del-valle-and-morales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benavente, Pobladura del Valle and Morales&lt;/a&gt;, which covers a substantial distance with notable climbs.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Benavente y Los Valles?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in the region are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037068130/tera-valley-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tera Valley Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the river valley, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036933830/moreruela-monastery-and-castrotorafe-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moreruela Monastery and Castrotorafe Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt;, combining natural beauty with historical sites.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Benavente y Los Valles is rich in history. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5509500" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benavente Castle – Tower of the Snail&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the magnificent palace-castle of the Pimentel family. Other historical points of interest include the Romanesque Church of Santa María del Azogue in Benavente and archaeological sites like dolmens. The region is also crossed by sections of the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3828668" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata&lt;/a&gt; and Camino Sanabrés pilgrimage routes.

Q: Are there any natural features or viewpoints to look out for?
A: Yes, the region offers diverse natural beauty. Cyclists can explore routes that pass by significant natural areas such as the Villafáfila and Villalpando Natural Reserve Lagoons, known for ornithology. The numerous river valleys, like the Río Esla, provide lush scenery. You might also encounter oak forests, particularly in lower elevations with Holm oak and higher areas with Pyrenean oak.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Benavente y Los Valles?
A: The touring cycling routes in Benavente y Los Valles are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the tranquility of the river valleys, and the opportunities to discover historical sites along the way.

Q: Are there routes that follow the Vía de la Plata?
A: Yes, the region is traversed by sections of the historic Vía de la Plata. You can find routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036970854/alija-del-infantado-from-benavente-circular-via-arrabalde-via-verde-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alija del Infantado from Benavente – circular via Arrabalde – Vía Verde de la Plata&lt;/a&gt;, which incorporates parts of this ancient path, offering a blend of history and scenic cycling.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Benavente y Los Valles?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the diverse landscapes and river valleys suggest that spring and autumn would likely offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling, with milder temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summers can be warm, and winters may be cooler, so checking local weather forecasts is always recommended.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by reservoirs or significant bridges?
A: Yes, you can find routes that include these features. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383275431/the-benavente-river-vegas-benavente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Benavente river vegas — Benavente&lt;/a&gt; route explores the river plains. You might also encounter landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4092040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de la Vizana&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/926254" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone Bridge over the Esla River&lt;/a&gt;, which are notable points of interest in the area.
tips:
One of the most beautiful and magnificent Cistercian monasteries in the province of Zamora is the Cistercian Monastery of Santa Maria, in the municipality of Granja de Moreruela.
The monastery preserves the size of the imposing Cistercian abbey ordered to be built by Alfonso VII. Built at the end of the 12th century, it is in the Romanesque style with elements of transition to Gothic.
The chancel is the largest part of the complex, formed by a superposition of levels: apses, a polygonal ambulatory and the central apse.
The walls of the Monastery are used by white storks to breed
Built in 1920 to serve the local community, the Quintos Bridge crosses the Esla River and the Ricobayo Reservoir.

We are inside the Castillo de Castrotorafe Castle, currently in ruins of what was a walled medieval city. The views of the Esla River, at the foot of the walls, are excellent. We will find information panels that will give us more information about the place.
Gravel section of the Vía de la Plata of the Camino de Santiago. During the rainy season the trail can become very muddy and difficult to bike.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular touring bicycle route, covering approximately 54.7 kilometers, generally takes around 4 hours and 13 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your cycling pace, stops for sightseeing, and the specific conditions of the day.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for touring cyclists on this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a varied terrain. You can expect a mix of dirt tracks, some with slight climbs, and rural roads. While sections of the Camino Sanabrés are part of the route, some parts of the official pilgrimage path can be quite uneven or rugged. For a smoother touring cycling experience, especially after the Quintos Bridge, you might consider using alternative provincial roads where available. There are also long, straight sections on dirt tracks and paths winding through fields.

faqs.question: What historical sites and landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in history. You'll have the opportunity to visit the impressive ruins of Castrotorafe Castle, a medieval fortification offering splendid views of the Esla River. You'll also cross the historic Quintos Bridge. An optional diversion can take you to the ruins of the 12th-century Cistercian Monastery of Santa María de Moreruela. The route also includes a gravel section of the Vía de la Plata (Camino de Santiago).

faqs.question: Where does the route start and where can I park in Granja de Moreruela?
faqs.answer: The route is circular, starting and ending in Granja de Moreruela. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Granja de Moreruela is a key starting point for the Camino Sanabrés, so you should find suitable parking options within the village, often near the main square or along less-trafficked streets.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during spring or autumn. These seasons offer more moderate temperatures, making the long, straight sections under the sun more comfortable. Summers can be very hot, and some sections might be exposed. Winter can bring colder, potentially wet conditions.

faqs.question: Does this cycling route follow any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several well-known paths. It follows parts of the Mtb Vía de la Plata, the Vía de la Plata hiking - 03 Salamanca to Astorga, the Camino Sanabrés, and the Mtb Camino Sanabrés (Este) / Mtb Camiño Xabrés (Leste).

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, cycling routes in rural Spain are dog-friendly, especially if your dog is accustomed to running alongside your bike or can be carried. However, given that parts of the route pass through agricultural lands and potentially near livestock, it's always best to keep your dog under control, preferably on a leash, to ensure their safety and respect local regulations and wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits are required to cycle this route. The trail passes through the Reserva Natural de las Lagunas de Villafáfila, but there are no general entrance fees for accessing the natural reserve itself. However, if you choose to visit the interior of historical sites like Castrotorafe Castle or the Monastery of Santa María de Moreruela, there might be a small entrance fee or specific visiting hours, so it's advisable to check ahead.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and wildlife can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience serene and solitary rural landscapes, characterized by vast fields, cultivated lands, and Mediterranean forests. Expect picturesque views of the Esla River, especially from the Quintos Bridge and from the vantage point of Castrotorafe Castle. Magnificent oak trees and cattle grazing in fenced pastures add to the charm. As the route is within the Reserva Natural de las Lagunas de Villafáfila, you might spot various bird species, especially waterfowl, and other local wildlife.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find accommodation near the route?
faqs.answer: Granja de Moreruela, being a starting point for the Camino Sanabrés, offers some basic services including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options suitable for pilgrims and cyclists. Moreruela de Tábara, another village on the route, also provides limited facilities. It's always a good idea to check opening hours, especially in smaller villages, or carry sufficient provisions.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners on a touring bicycle?
faqs.answer: While the overall difficulty is rated as moderate, some sections of the Camino Sanabrés can be quite uneven or rugged, which might be challenging for beginners or those on touring bikes not equipped for rougher terrain. If you're a beginner, consider sticking to the provincial road alternatives mentioned in the terrain description for a more comfortable experience. Good physical condition and some cycling experience are recommended for the full route as mapped.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations to be aware of since the route passes through the Reserva Natural de las Lagunas de Villafáfila?
faqs.answer: When cycling through the Reserva Natural de las Lagunas de Villafáfila, it's important to stay on marked paths to protect the natural environment and wildlife, especially nesting birds. Avoid disturbing animals, do not litter, and respect any signage regarding protected areas or specific regulations. While no specific cycling permits are needed, responsible behavior is expected to preserve the natural beauty of the reserve.
